figures.caption: Table 4: ONNX-to-text Encoding vs. PyTorch Text (NAS-Bench-201).
Table 5: Derivation Tree String vs. PyTorch Text (einspace).
Figure 8: The PyTorch code representation for the einspace architecture shown in Figure 7.

Figure 4: NAS trajectory on CIFAR-10 (top), CIFAR-100 (middle), and ImageNet16-120 (bottom) in NAS-Bench-201 (100 trials each). A line graph shows Average Best-Found (Test or Validation) Accuracy vs. Number of Architectures Evaluated. The comparison between COLE (green) vs path encodings (red) with the BANANAS algorithm are shown. Colored shading indicates a 95% confidence interval, and vertical gray shading indicates stages at which COLE’s performance is statistically significantly different.

Figure 5: An excerpt of the Backbone add-on optionally used in COLE. The implementations for the referenced ResNetBasicblock and its dependency, the ReLUConvBN operation, are not shown here but are included in the PyTorch code string when the Backbone add-on is used.
Figure 6: The Comment add-on optionally used in COLE. A summary of the cell, macroskeleton (Backbone), task, and training details is provided.
Figure 7: The derivation tree string representation for a sample einspace architecture.

Figure 2: A sample code representation under the Helper Method mode. Complex operations are fully defined as separate classes (e.g., ReLU_Conv2d_BatchNorm) and instantiated within the Cell. The Excluded Helper Method mode would simply omit the separate class from the string.

Figure 11: t-SNE visualizations comparing derivation tree strings and COLE on a 2,837-architecture corpus. The layout follows Figure 9, filtering and recomputing the projection for the top 20% of architectures. CL: CodeLlama.

Figure 10: t-SNE visualizations comparing ONNX-to-text encodings with COLE on a 7,400-architecture subset of NAS-Bench-201. The layout follows Figure 9, filtering and recomputing the projection for the top 10% of architectures. Left column: architectures having accuracy ≤20% are painted the same color. CL: CodeLlama.

Table 6: Average best-found accuracy (± standard deviation) values from the NAS trajectories shown in Figure 4 at different evaluation budgets. The accuracy metric reported for the CIFAR-10 end-task is validation accuracy, while test accuracy is reported for CIFAR-100 and ImageNet16-120.
Figure 3: The Inline mode code representation. The logic for complex operations are defined inline directly inside the __init__method using the nn.Sequentialmodule.

Figure 9: t-SNE visualizations comparing structural encodings with COLE on NAS-Bench-201. The left column shows the full search space. The middle column filters for the top 10% of architectures and plots using the original t-SNE projection. The right column recomputes the t-SNE projection on the top 10%. Left column: architectures having accuracy ≤20% are painted the same color.
Table 3: Model comparison (Kendall’s Tau) across different training set sizes. CL: CodeLlama; MB: ModernBERT
Table 1: Average Kendall’s Tau (𝜏) across different training set sizes when modifying the Base configuration.
